name: Congo, Republic of the
excerpt: Upon independence in 1960, the former French region of Middle Congo became the Republic of the Congo. From 1968 to 1992, the country was named the People’s Republic of the Congo.

quick_facts.religions: Roman Catholic 33.1%, Awakening Churches/Christian Revival 22.3%, Protestant 19.9%, Salutiste 2.2%, Muslim 1.6%, Kimbanguist 1.5%, other 8.1%, none 11.3% (2007 est.)
quick_facts.ethnic_groups: Kongo (Bakongo) 40.5%, Teke 16.9%, Mbochi 13.1%, foreigner 8.2%, Sangha 5.6%, Mbere/Mbeti/Kele 4.4%, Punu 4.3%, Pygmy 1.6%, Oubanguiens 1.6%, Duma 1.5%, Makaa 1.3%, other and unspecified 1% (2014-15 est.)
quick_facts.currency: Cooperation Financiere en Afrique Centrale francs (XAF)
quick_facts.climate: tropical; rainy season (March to June); dry season (June to October); persistent high temperatures and humidity; particularly enervating climate astride the Equator
quick_facts.terrain: coastal plain, southern basin, central plateau, northern basin
quick_facts.natural_resources: petroleum, timber, potash, lead, zinc, uranium, copper, phosphates, gold, magnesium, natural gas, hydropower
quick_facts.major_exports: crude petroleum, refined copper, gold, wood, refined petroleum (2023)

summaries.geography_summary: about 70% of the population lives in Brazzaville, Pointe-Noire, or along the railroad between them
summaries.history_summary: Upon independence in 1960, the former French region of Middle Congo became the Republic of the Congo. From 1968 to 1992, the country was named the People’s Republic of the Congo. A quarter-century of experimentation with Marxism was abandoned in 1990, and a democratically elected government took office in 1992, at which time the country reverted to "the Republic of the Congo" name. A two-year civil war that ended in 1999 restored to power former President Denis SASSOU-Nguesso, who had ruled from 1979 to 1992. A new constitution adopted three years later provided for a multi-party system and a seven-year presidential term, and the next elections retained SASSOU-Nguesso. After a year of renewed fighting, SASSOU-Nguesso and southern-based rebel groups agreed to a final peace accord in 2003. SASSOU-Nguesso was reelected in 2009 and, after passing a constitutional referendum allowing him to run for additional terms, was reelected again in 2016 and 2021. The Republic of the Congo is one of Africa's largest petroleum producers.
summaries.economy_summary: primarily an oil- and natural resources-based economy; recovery from mid-2010s oil devaluation has been slow and curtailed by COVID-19; extreme poverty increasing, particularly in southern rural regions; attempting to implement recommended CEMAC reforms; increasing likelihood of debt default

suggested_reading: CIA World Factbook data (archived via factbook.json) | https://github.com/factbook/factbook.json

prayer_and_mission.prayer_intro: The Republic of the Congo has a substantial Protestant and independent evangelical presence alongside its Catholic plurality. Pray for these churches to be strengthened in sound biblical doctrine.
prayer_points: Pray for the Republic of the Congo's Protestant and independent evangelical churches to be equipped with sound, Scripture-centered doctrine.
Pray for pastors to be trained in expository preaching.
Pray for Catholics practicing inherited religious tradition to encounter genuine salvation by grace through faith in Christ.
prayer_and_mission.mission_emphasis: The Republic of the Congo's substantial Protestant and independent evangelical presence is real ground to build on. Ongoing needs include training national pastors in sound biblical doctrine and reaching the country's Catholic population with the clear gospel.
